description_long: This is a military-style, single-propeller airplane with a streamlined fuselage painted in dark green and gray camouflage. The wings are straight and mid-mounted, each bearing roundel insignias typical of British Royal Air Force aircraft. The tail section features a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, and there is a visible tailwheel. The cockpit is positioned forward on the fuselage and appears to be enclosed, with a clear canopy. The engine cowling is rounded and houses a single piston engine with a two-blade propeller at the nose. The airplane's surface is matte, with painted insignias and markings, and the landing gear is of the taildragger type.
description: A dark green and gray military-style single-propeller airplane with mid-mounted straight wings, roundel insignias, and a conventional tail. The cockpit is forward and enclosed, and the landing gear is a taildragger configuration.
description_view_0: Oblique front-left view showing the propeller, forward cockpit, and roundel insignias on the wings.
description_view_1: Direct front view highlighting the propeller, symmetrical wing span, and the forward cockpit canopy.
description_view_2: Oblique front-right view displaying the engine cowling, cockpit, and tail section with visible markings.
description_view_3: Direct rear view focusing on the tail assembly, horizontal stabilizers, and the tailwheel.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with straight wings and no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single piston engine mounted in the nose with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two main wheels and a single tailwheel, fixed gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single-seat canopy integrated into the fuselage.
